examples.title: Basic File Writing
examples.description: Demonstrates how to create and write to a text file using ofstream
examples.explanation: Creates ofstream object with filename, checks if file opened successfully using is_open(), writes using << operator, and closes file properly.

examples.title: Basic File Reading
examples.description: Demonstrates how to read from a text file using ifstream
examples.explanation: Creates ifstream object, uses getline() in a while loop to read line by line until EOF, closes file. getline() returns false at EOF.

examples.title: Appending to File
examples.description: Shows how to append data to existing file without overwriting
examples.explanation: Opens file with ios::app mode, which positions write pointer at end of file. All new data is added after existing content.

examples.title: Reading Word by Word
examples.description: Demonstrates reading tokens separated by whitespace
examples.explanation: Uses extraction operator >> which reads whitespace-separated tokens. The while condition automatically checks for EOF.

examples.title: Using fstream for Read and Write
examples.description: Shows how to open file for both input and output operations
examples.explanation: fstream opened with ios::in | ios::out allows both reading and writing. seek reposition the file pointer between operations.

examples.title: File Copy Program
examples.description: Copies contents from one file to another
examples.explanation: Opens source in read mode, destination in write mode. Uses getline() to read line by line and writes each to destination.
